articles.title: Kochi Corporation in Kerala faces huge biomedical waste backlog as incinerator capacity is reduced
articles.description: One of two incinerators at Common Bio-Medical Waste Treatment Facility of KEIL remained shut for 15 days last month. Though the incinerator is back in operation, KEIL has restricted intake to three tonnes a day, down from five tonnes, as it prioritises hospital waste. One of two incinerators at Brahmapuram, with a combined capacity of three tonnes a day, developed a snag during its trial run

articles.description: One of two incinerators at Common Bio-Medical Waste Treatment Facility of KEIL remained shut for 15 days last month. Though the incinerator is back in operation, KEIL has restricted intake to three tonnes a day, down from five tonnes, as it prioritises hospital waste. One of two incinerators at Brahmapuram, with a combined capacity of three tonnes a day, developed a snag during its trial run

articles.title: Which country has the world's longest undersea railway tunnel?
articles.description: Which country has the worlds longest undersea railway tunnel

articles.title: After Trump, Netanyahu issues 'no life insurance' warning to Mojtaba Khamenei: 'We’re not waiting'
articles.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned Iran's new supreme leader, Ayotallah Mojtaba Khamenei, that Jerusalem is ‘initiating, attacking’. This follows US President Donald Trump's warning of potential elimination if Mojtaba does not comply with demands regarding Tehran's nuclear programme.

articles.title: US tariff relief adds sparkle to Indian diamond trade as polishers eye 6-7% revenue growth; Mideast conflict a concern
articles.description: Indian diamond polishers are expected to achieve 6-7% revenue growth in fiscal 2027, aided by a reduction in US tariffs, which is set to boost exports. Details here.

articles.title: 4 crew aboard refueling aircraft crashed in Iraq killed, says US Central Command, denies hostile or friendly fire
articles.description: Four crew members of a KC-135 refueling aircraft have died following its crash in western Iraq. The US military stated the loss was not due to hostile or friendly fire, as investigations continue amidst heightened military activity in the region.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Maya Angelou: ‘Do the best you can until you know better. Then when you know better, do better’
articles.description: Maya Angelou transformed her painful childhood into powerful literature. Awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom, her teachings on personal growth resonate even today, promoting that learning from past mistakes leads to continuous improvement and resilience in the face of challenges.

topStories.description: A 66-year-old man in Punjab's Barnala district allegedly died of a heart attack while waiting for an LPG cylinder for 2 hours amid supply shortages due to the Middle East conflict.  A Congress leader blamed the government for the tragic circumstances.

topStories.title: Metal stocks slide up to 7% as dollar hits 4-month high; NALCO, Hindustan Copper lead fall
topStories.description: Metal stocks, including NALCO and Hindustan Copper fell sharply on Friday as a surging US Dollar Index and ongoing Iran–Israel/US conflict dented demand. Analysts advise caution, noting that global commodity prices and infrastructure trends will shape near-term metal sector performance.

dailyNews.description: By suing Republican states and making sharp reversals in old cases, the Trump administration is using courts to fast-track major shifts in policy.

dailyNews.title: Trump Targets Forced Labor in Global Tariff Scheme
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration began a trade investigation Thursday into whether dozens of countries have policies to combat forced labor.

dailyNews.title: Canada Plans Military Expansion in Arctic, Following Trump Threats
dailyNews.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada would build three bases in the region. The government also plans to improve infrastructure and airports in the north.

dailyNews.title: Iran war disrupts global oil trade as Trump administration prepares to escort ships
dailyNews.description: Iran's relentless attacks on Gulf states and infrastructure appear to be overshadowing interventions by the U.S. and its allies aimed at easing energy prices.

dailyNews.title: 14 million doses of fentanyl  seized from secret drug lab in Mexico
dailyNews.description: The raids come as President Donald Trump ramps up his criticism of Mexico's record on fighting drug trafficking.

dailyNews.title: U.S. launches new investigations into 60 countries as it fights to restore tariffs
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has launched investigations into dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, flexing a law that lets the federal government impose tariffs.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration allows purchase of Russian oil already at sea
dailyNews.description: The U.S. is temporarily allowing the purchase of Russian oil that's already at sea, in the Trump administration's latest move to loosen sanctions on Russia's oil industry as the world grapples with high oil prices.

dailyNews.title: Experts, reports on the Strait of Hormuz and gas prices
dailyNews.description: Ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz was mostly stopped amid the war in Iran on Thursday, which contributed to rising oil prices. The waterway typically carries around 20% of the world's supply of oil. On Wednesday, President Trump ordered the release of 172 million barrels of oil from emergency reserves. In a statement attributed to Iran's new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, he said Iran should keep putting pressure on the critical chokepoint.

dailyNews.title: Cuba to release 51 people from prison in surprise move amid Trump pressure
dailyNews.description: Cuba's government says it will release 51 people from prisons, in an unexpected move that comes as the Trump administration puts immense pressure on the country.

dailyNews.title: Oil holds above $100 as Trump says America ‘has ammunition and plenty of time’ to fight Iran war
dailyNews.description: WTI crude saw its biggest weekly gain since the 1980s last week.

dailyNews.title: Trump-backed SAVE America Act will get a Senate vote next week, Thune says
dailyNews.description: Advocates of the voter-ID bill have pressed the Senate for months to call a vote on the controversial legislation.
